title: Responsive CSS, Reflow, Progressive Enhancement, and Performance Budgets · Language Clinic: Resource Budget
summary: Generated pages assume one viewport, content clips at zoom, navigation depends on JavaScript, fonts block text, images shift layout, and a fast home page hides slow long-form routes. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the resource budget increment in responsive progressively enhanced performance budget. Learners progress through prediction, explic…
objectives: Budget HTML, CSS, JavaScript, fonts, images, requests, layout shifts, and critical rendering work per representative template and route.
Deliver core reading and navigation in HTML, layer CSS and optional JavaScript, and define failure behavior for blocked or broken enhancements.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Predict Resource Budget · Theory Resource Budget
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory publishing team handles case 4063 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate representative pages on reflow, progressive enhancement, and resource budgets; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example publishing team handles case 4063 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate representative pages on reflow, progressive enhancement, and resource budgets; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Budget HTML, CSS, JavaScript, fonts, images, requests, layout shifts, and critical rendering work per representative template and route. A defensible solution applies Resource Budget, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to responsive progressively enhanced performance budget.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-responsive-performance-3
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-responsive-performance-3.
# Case 5798: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def worked_static_site_responsive_performance_3(viewports: int = 3, reflow_passes: int = 3, page_kib: int = 180, page_budget_kib: int = 220, layout_shifts: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "readerworked3",
        "source5798",
        "parse3dekrow",
        "routeworked35798",
        "artifact5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if viewports < 3 or reflow_passes < 0:
        return False, reflow_passes, "viewport-matrix"
    if reflow_passes != viewports:
        return False, reflow_passes, "reflow-failure"
    if page_kib < 0 or page_kib > page_budget_kib:
        return False, page_kib, "resource-budget"
    if layout_shifts > 0:
        return False, layout_shifts, "layout-instability"
    assert reflow_passes == viewports
    return True, page_kib, "responsive-budget"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Build Resource Budget evidence · Theory Resource Budget
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-resource-budget-1e21zi" that uses case 1039 to gate representative pages on reflow, progressive enhancement, and resource budgets. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Build Resource Budget evidence · Theory Resource Budget.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Resource Budget into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Build Resource Budget evidence · Theory Resource Budget, keep Resource Budget connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-resource-budget-1e21zi
# Competency: ssg-resource-budget.
# Case 1039: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def evidence_ssg_resource_budget_1e21zi(viewports: int = 3, reflow_passes: int = 3, page_kib: int = 180, page_budget_kib: int = 220, layout_shifts: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1e21zi = "1e21zi-iz12e1-1039"
    assert evidence_variant_1e21zi
    evidence_axes_1e21zi = (
        "reader1e21zi",
        "source1039",
        "parseiz12e1",
        "route1e21zi1039",
        "artifact10391e21zi",
        "recoveryiz12e11039",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1e21zi)) == 6
    if viewports < 3 or reflow_passes < 0:
        return False, reflow_passes, "viewport-matrix"
    if reflow_passes != viewports:
        return False, reflow_passes, "reflow-failure"
    if page_kib < 0 or page_kib > page_budget_kib:
        return False, page_kib, "resource-budget"
    if layout_shifts > 0:
        return False, layout_shifts, "layout-instability"
    assert reflow_passes == viewports
    return True, page_kib, "responsive-budget"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Resource Budget model · Theory Resource Budget
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Resource Budget: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 5873 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate representative pages on reflow, progressive enhancement, and resource budgets; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Budget HTML, CSS, JavaScript, fonts, images, requests, layout shifts, and critical rendering work per representative template and route.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Resource Budget model · Theory Resource Budget.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Resource Budget model · Theory Resource Budget, keep Resource Budget connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Budget HTML, CSS, JavaScript, fonts, images, requests, layout shifts, and critical rendering work per representative template and route.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Resource Budget model · Theory Resource Budget, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Resource Budget precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Resource Budget model · Theory Resource Budget, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 5873 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate representative pages on reflow, progressive enhancement, and resource budgets; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Budget HTML, CSS, JavaScript, fonts, images, requests, layout shifts, and critical rendering work per representative template and route.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Resource Budget model · Theory Resource Budget, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: One total transfer-size limit captures user-visible performance.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: ssg-resource-budget-1e21zi.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-resource-budget-1e21zi(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_resource_budget_1e21zi\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?viewports < 3 or reflow_passes < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?reflow_passes != viewports)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?page_kib < 0 or page_kib > page_budget_kib)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?layout_shifts > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
